one_time_description: NWF Resources LP Macro pipeline agreement revenue of $9,731,891 representing a significant one-time/non-recurring pipeline-related payment
economic_notes: Nation has significant economic development activity including gas bar operations (Tse Yaz Bunket Gas Bar), Nadleh Store, camp services through Horizon/Falcon, pipeline agreements with NWF Resources LP and Coastal Gaslink, and equity investments in multiple limited partnerships including Lhai Ventures LP and Endako Tsey LP. Defined contribution pension plan at 5% of gross payroll.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Large spike in revenue driven by NWF Resources LP Macro pipeline agreement ($9.7M) and Province of BC transfers ($11.6M) causing annual surplus to jump from $3.9M to $24.8M. One fully consolidated subsidiary (Tse Yaz Bunket Gas Bar Ltd.) and 9 entities on modified equity basis (4 GBEs and 5 government business partnerships). Ottawa Trust Fund of $1.77M included as financial asset. No schedule of remuneration disclosed. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($3,940,983) + Province of BC ($11,581,846) + First Nations Health Authority ($708,972) + Ottawa Trust Fund income ($430,091) + Carrier Sekani Family Services ($986,806) + Carrier Sekani Tribal Council ($94,611) + First Nations Education Steering Committee ($168,492) + other indigenous groups remainder = approximate; OSR calculated as total revenue minus identifiable government transfers. Asset retirement obligation of $100,000 recognized in 2023 for asbestos removal. Audit report signed December 5, 2024.
